Alright, welcome back. So hopefully that last video you're like, all right, I don't have to be a perfectionist about being on camera. And I'll show you how to completely avoid being on camera. If you want to, you can literally just have words on the screen works pretty good. Let's just get something going. But the something that we get going needs to be done pretty well in terms of what you're saying the messaging. So we're going to start with this right here, which is the YouTube ad script framework for folks who are selling info products like courses and stuff. Or if you're selling services like an agency or anything, or trying to get people on a webinar. Alright, so really this is best. Like if your funnel or your sales processes, they opt in, they see a sales pitch, they get some follow ups and they buy or they don't. Right, so this is different from like, if you've got a $4 bundle of stuff or whatever and say, hey, go buy it. This is a different script. So this is really a lead gen script for folks who are trying to do something of that nature. Okay, and it works like a champ. Alright, so let's go through it. Now, notice this in our first five seconds, what we want to do is we want to hook them and pre sell them on watching the video but stop, please. Wait a minute. You know, in marketing, if you're not paying for someone to watch the video, then it's fun to do all this pattern interrupt stuff and build up all this curiosity and everything on YouTube. We don't want to do that. Okay, what we want to do, this is in our first five seconds, say, hey, this video is going to show you three ways to lose belly fat or whatever. And that's it. So it's not like, notice, I've got a cat here. What's the cat doing? Am I going to juggle it? Watch the video and find out. Like, don't do that because you'll get totally unqualified random people watching your video and it'll cost you money. Okay, so that's our first five seconds. The reason we want to do that is because we want them to hit the skip button. All right, if they're not interested in your topic, like whatever your video is about, if they're not interested in getting the result that your video is going to help them get to, then you want them to hit skip. No idea why that happened in the background. I don't know if I hopefully I was able to edit out that music, but this video just popped up from where my other video was where I was showing you my ad. Sorry about that. Anyway, you want them to hit skip. Okay, I know that's weird, but if they hit skip, you don't pay. Right? So there's no point in paying to have someone watch the video if they don't want to watch the video if they're not your perfect person. So we want to hook them. This is what this video is about. That's it. And if they have no interest in that, great. So it's really, really simple. Right? So we're going to say something like, Hey, everybody, my name is Rick James. And in this video, I'm going to show you three ways to play bass guitar like Bootsy Collins. Let's go. All right, so that was probably under six seconds. Okay, so that's what you're doing. Really, really, really, really simple. So our next five through 30 seconds, give or take, right? So you don't need to have a timer out. The big thing here is if they skip the video before 30 seconds is up, you don't pay. So essentially what we want to do is after we like hook up like, Hey, here's why you want to watch this video, we want to attract and repel. All right, so we want to tell them who the video is for. And you can even do that from the very, very beginning. So like in some of the examples that I'll show you of my stuff, I'll say, Hey, you know, if you have an online business and you currently sell things and your ads are stuck, this video is going to show you how to fix it. I'm doing that like all in the first 30 seconds. Right. So you can reverse the order in which you're doing this. It doesn't matter. But let's just say you're following it just verbatim, which, you know, if you're, if you're a rules based person, you're like, give me the damn blueprint, then just do it. Exactly. Right. So let's say, for example, all right, we're selling, you know, marketing consulting stuff. All right, so I might say, Hey, everybody, Frank Kern here in this video, you're going to learn three critical ways to double any business without spending any money on ads. All right. Blam. All right, so that let's just say right there. That's got them hooked. They're like, Oh, okay. Interesting. Right. It was under six seconds. So now what I want to do is I want to attract the ones that I want. I want to repel the ones I don't, which is going to be within that five to 30 second period. If it's shorter than 30 seconds, it doesn't matter. We just want to make sure it's not longer than 30 seconds before we repel the people. All right. And it just, you can't seem to break a revenue barrier. So that's who this is for. If that's you, you're going to love this. Now, if you have no business at all, and you're looking to start a business, you're going to hate this video and out of respect for your time, you should go ahead and hit the skip button on this because it will bore you. I think writing scripts is going to drive you crazy. I think it's going to be horrible and you're going to sound canned. What I would rather have you do is literally just have this. And even like if you just turn on your laptop camera, for example, and you have this on, and you have this on the screen, and you just say the little piece. So that's literally why I'm not having you write a script. Sounds canned. They're going to be able to tell. You don't want to look scripted. You want to look like you know what you're talking about. Okay, so our next phase here from 30 to 120 seconds ish is we want to educate them. So you've heard me say this a billion times. The greatest way you can convince somebody that you can help them is by actually helping them. So this framework is for someone who sells info products or services or wants to get people on a webinar that's going to show them how to achieve some goal. Right. So we want them to actually do it. And the best way to do that is by giving them results in advance, which is like, you know, the trick I've been teaching forever. It just works. It's like, let's, you know, use the oldest trick in the book here. Let's demonstrate we can help them by actually helping them. And there's a couple of ways to do it. All right. So one angle is contrarian, which is everything you've learned is wrong. Here's why. Right. And so if we look at, let's see if we can find this video ad from me. Oh man, hang on. I'm going to bring up that awful video that where I look terrible. Hang on just a second. So in this particular video, right around the 90 second mark, what I'm doing is I'm taking this angle, right? So I'm saying, okay, look, you know, if you're stuffing work and here's why I'm going to draw it out for you on this, you know, digital whiteboard thingy here. And I'm like, okay, if your ads aren't working, here's what you're hearing about ads and it's wrong. And I'm saying this is what you're hearing. So in this video, I'm essentially saying, all right, people are like all obsessed over either targeting, or they're obsessed over the platform, or they're obsessed over some sort of a tactic or whatever. And all of that's completely wrong. What that is, is the contrarian angle, right? So we could just stick with that for your education part and then just give evidence to it. Right. So a lot of things that you might see from me is when I love doing this, by the way, which is where I'm like, hey, you know, everyone's saying that you have to have perfect targeting on ads. I mean, good gosh, you've got to be committed to watch something like this, right? So anyway, that's my point. Now, if we come over here to this framework again, our second way. Is the new way versus old way, right? So we could do this easily. I could do this with the new way of advertising versus the old way where I demonstrate the old way, which is like, okay, here's the old way. And if your ads aren't working, it's probably because you're doing it the old way. Number one, you've heard that you've got to make the perfect ad. Number two, and I've actually made ads like this is why it's so easy for me to say. Number two, you've heard that you have to have the perfect targeting. And number three, you've heard that you have to have everything just on the right platform. So people will say, well, Facebook is better or YouTube is better or whatever. And I'm here to tell you right now, that's the old way. And here's why. All right. And then I would tell them why like, okay, here's what's wrong with this whole concept of the perfect ad. You're going to spend all this time trying to make the perfect ad. And then you're never really going to know which one works. What you really need to do is make a bunch of them that you think are pretty good and really obvious for who you want and then let the machine learning take over. And that would be part of the new way machine learning. Now say, okay, let's talk about this targeting people are going to say you have to find your customer avatar and all that. And they're completely wrong. That's the old way. The new way is to understand, right, that Facebook, YouTube, Instagram, whoever, they've got algorithms and they're analyzing over 10,000 data points on every single human. So they're able to find your customers a lot better than you can if you just tell them, or if you just give them enough time to do it, etc. Right. And we might even like draw that out like new way on one side, old way versus another. So that's another great way to do the education part of the ad itself. All right. And I've given you some examples here, you know, we could do and I actually plan to do this with our software which is create by mech which is to go right after the automated webinar industry because everybody hates it. So I'll be like, if you're running ads to automated webinars and they're not working, here's why is the old way of doing it. Nobody likes it and everybody knows that it's fake. Think about this. You're on Facebook, you're scrolling, you see an ad for a webinar, you want to sign up, you register, now you have to wait at least 15 minutes. So you go back on Facebook, you get distracted and you don't show up for the webinar. Number two, people aren't stupid. They know that all of those comments that are coming on there is not real people on the webinar. They understand these things are completely automated. So right there, you've got two things going against you. You're paying for people to register and the ones who actually do show up, which most don't, but the ones who do, you're already breaking their trust. You know, that's the old way of doing it. If I were to say to anybody who's running those types of promotions, I'd say, hey, 2009 called and they want their marketing strategies back. Let me show you what works better instead. It's the new way, which is simply an on-demand class and here's how it works. I don't really know what that means. They're fixing stuff that ain't working right, but it's pretty cool. But you get it, right? You understand. And then, angle number three is a classic, three simple steps. So we don't even have to get into all that stuff. So here are three simple ways to increase your business. Way number one, have a little bit better opt-in rate. Way number two, have a little bit better conversion rate from the people who opt-in. And so you teach just a big picture thing. So if we were saying, have a better opt-in rate, if your opt-in rate's at 20%, let's see what we get at 25%. I actually recently did a video about this on how to double any business. And it was literally little incremental changes like that. So if I said, okay, let's go from 20% to 28% or 25% or whatever, here are some ways to do it. And I might say, okay, here's a bad opt-in page and I'd show them a bad design and then I'd show them something like this. Here's a page that converts at about 40%. This is ridiculous. Who knows why it converts like this? Here's one that also converts at around 40%. Notice the similarities. It's a big sentence plus a button. Try that. It might work pretty good. So that's way number one that I might say, okay, next thing we want to do is we want to increase the conversion rate, not on your sales page itself necessarily, but the amount of people that actually end up buying in total. How do we do that? Well, let's start with your email sequences. Here's a type of email sequence that almost always increases sales. And then I might say, number three, we could increase the average order value of every customer by adding order bumps or one-click up sales or whatever. You get it. So really just really freaking basic. And here's the deal on this thing. You literally want to give big picture stuff. So I wouldn't, if I were to say, step number one, I want to teach these three steps in two minutes or something. I don't want to go forever. I want them to actually opt in for the webinar or do whatever it is. The next step is. So I might literally be like, step number one, you want to increase your opt-in rate. Here's what a bad page looks like. Here's what a good page looks like. Here's why this is good. I wouldn't be like, here's how to use click funnels or here's how to do HTML or whatever. And for the email sequencing, I'll be like, all right, here's what most people do. They put them in a nurture sequence. Here's what works really good. It's called a deadline sequence. Here's the way you do it. Generally, you set an amount of time, like four or six hours, and you send one email every hour up until the last hour before they can buy. Generally, that causes people to buy more. Like simple stuff. Don't over-complicate this. So we've educated, right? We've delivered a little bit of results in advance. That's all. You don't have to overthink this thing. So that's what you do. So now, after 120 seconds-ish, right? So there's no firm limit on this. I mean, this thing is seven minutes long for crying out loud.
